name: Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail
name_original: Castroceniza desde Ura — circular por el Desfiladero de Mataviejas
description: Hike the moderate 6.4-mile (10.4 km) Castroceniza from Ura circular route through Desfiladero de Mataviejas, featuring gorges and vultures.
region_intro: Hiking around Covarrubias explores a landscape characterized by river valleys, such as the Mataviejas Gorge, and forested hills. The region features a mix of rolling terrain and elevated viewpoints, offering diverse hiking experiences. Trails often follow waterways and ascend to panoramic vistas over the Castilian countryside. Best hiking trails around Covarrubias The most popular hiking route is Mataviejas Gorge Trail, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This easy path follows the Mataviejas Gorge, offering a gentle walk. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de las Mamblas — circular desde Covarrubias, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path. This route provides a circular journey through the Sierra de las Mamblas, featuring significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through the Mataviejas Gorge and surrounding woodlands, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Covarrubias is defined by river gorges, forested hills, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Covarrubias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Covarrubias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Covarrubias?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Covarrubias, catering to various skill levels. This includes 7 easy routes, 33 moderate routes, and 3 difficult trails for more experienced hikers.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Covarrubias?
A: Yes, Covarrubias offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281815249/mataviejas-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.5 miles (5.6 km) long and follows the river, offering a gentle and scenic walk.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes around Covarrubias?
A: Hikes around Covarrubias vary significantly. You can find shorter, easier paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281815249/mataviejas-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 1 hour 28 minutes. For more challenging, longer excursions, routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042240105/mirador-de-las-penas-del-tuero-por-retuerta-circular-desde-covarrubias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Penas del Tuero por Retuerta — circular desde Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; can be over 12 miles (20 km) and take around 5 hours 40 minutes.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Covarrubias?
A: Yes, many trails around Covarrubias are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281950750/sierra-de-las-mamblas-circular-desde-covarrubias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Mamblas — circular desde Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the hiking trails in Covarrubias?
A: The hiking trails in Covarrubias offer diverse scenery, characterized by river valleys like the Mataviejas Gorge, forested hills, and elevated viewpoints. You'll experience a mix of rolling terrain and vistas over the Castilian countryside, often following waterways and ascending to scenic lookouts.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking around Covarrubias, you can explore several historical and natural landmarks. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4659990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; itself, and various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering great views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Covarrubias?
A: The hiking routes in Covarrubias are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents, and the beautiful natural landscapes of river gorges and forested hills.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs on the trails around Covarrubias?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Covarrubias region. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock, and to carry waste bags. Always check for specific local regulations or signage at trailheads.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Covarrubias?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Covarrubias are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, with comfortable temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes that offer good views?
A: Yes, for a moderate challenge with rewarding views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282042421/mataviejas-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.5 miles (10.4 km) route takes you through the gorge and surrounding woodlands, offering scenic vistas and a good workout, typically completed in under 3 hours.

Q: Where can I find parking when hiking in Covarrubias?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Covarrubias. For specific trailheads outside the village, look for designated parking areas, which are usually indicated. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to a summit or high viewpoint?
A: Yes, several trails in the region lead to elevated points with panoramic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281950750/sierra-de-las-mamblas-circular-desde-covarrubias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Mamblas — circular desde Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; route involves significant elevation gain, leading to higher ground where you can enjoy expansive views over the surrounding landscape.
tips: A pretty town where the Mataviejas River passes through, with its spectacular canyon

The Mataviejas river forms a beautiful gorge between the towns of Castroceniza and Ura. The walk is easy and pleasant, accessible also for children. It is recommended to visit this unknown but very interesting place. In summer, it is essential to carry water, since the path is exposed and without shade.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Castroceniza from Ura trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is generally considered moderate. While it features gentle ascents and descents and is largely straightforward, its length of over 10 km and duration of around 3 hours (as per komoot data) place it beyond a simple stroll. However, many find it an easy and pleasant route, suitable for families, due to the well-marked paths and lack of significant technical challenges.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows a narrow, winding path alongside the Mataviejas River. You'll encounter a mix of well-marked footpaths and some forest tracks. The route is comfortable with gentle changes in elevation, but no steep inclines.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Castroceniza from Ura hike?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the charming village of Ura, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Castroceniza also has an informational sign and a fountain at the route's beginning, suggesting it could be an alternative starting point with parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Castroceniza from Ura trail family-friendly and dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered suitable for families and children due to its generally easy and pleasant nature. It is also dog-friendly, making it a great option for hiking with your canine companion. Always ensure dogs are kept under control, especially given the potential for wildlife sightings.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Autumn is particularly vibrant, with poplars and ash trees displaying rich colors. Spring offers lush greenery and pleasant temperatures. Summers can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winters might require appropriate gear depending on conditions.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The central attraction is the impressive Mataviejas Gorge, with its spectacular rock formations, cliffs, and natural caves. You'll also pass through the traditional villages of Ura and Castroceniza. Keep an eye out for remnants of an ancient Celtiberian fort and castle ruins near the "La Serna" area, which offer panoramic views.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the Mataviejas Gorge?
faqs.answer: The cliffs of the gorge are home to a significant colony of griffon vultures, offering excellent opportunities for birdwatching. Egyptian vultures have also been sighted in the area. The diverse vegetation, including sabinas, holm oaks, and walnut trees, supports various other bird species and small mammals.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for hiking in the Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail within the Parque Natural de Sabinares del Arlanza-La Yecla. However, always respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and follow any local regulations or signage you may encounter.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other local paths. You will find yourself on parts of the "Desfiladero del Mataviejas (de Ura a Castroceniza)" and "de Ura a Castroceniza" trails, as well as segments of "Calle Real" and "Calle la Iglesia" within the villages.

faqs.question: What is a unique highlight or insider tip for this hike?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning gorge, make sure to look for the remnants of the Celtiberian fort and castle ruins near the "La Serna" area. These offer truly spectacular panoramic views of the canyon and valley, providing a historical perspective alongside the natural beauty. Also, keep an eye out for the discrete wooden markers along the trail, which provide valuable ecological and cultural information.
